description: A rocketry club needs to fly a standard 41.6mm (BT-60) payload experiment, but their only available launch vehicle is a smaller 25.4mm (BT-50) rocket. Modify the 'simple_model_rocket.ork' design to carry this payload by replacing the original nose cone with a transition (Aft OD: 25.4mm, Fore OD: 41.6mm, Length: ~40mm), a payload bay body tube (OD: 41.6mm, Length: ~150mm), a 50g mass component representing the experiment, and a new 41.6mm base nose cone. The modified rocket must be stabilized to achieve a stability margin of at least 1.0 caliber. Run a flight simulation using an appropriate motor (such as an Estes C6-3 or C6-5) to ensure a successful flight. Save the modified design to '~/Documents/rockets/payload_lofter.ork' and write a summary report documenting the final stability margin, the motor used, and the predicted apogee altitude to '~/Documents/exports/expansion_report.txt'.
